input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
Here's how to make your page theme-aware:

## XAML â€” `AppThemeBinding`

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page follows the system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#AAAAAA}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

`AppThemeBinding` listens to the OS theme and swaps values automatically â€” no event handling needed.

## C# Alternative â€” `SetAppTheme`

The equivalent in code-behind or C# markup:

```csharp
// On any BindableObject, call SetAppTheme:
myLabel.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty,
    Colors.Black,   // light value
    Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0")); // dark value

BackgroundColor = Application.Current!.RequestedTheme == AppTheme.Dark
    ? Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E")
    : Colors.White;

// SetAppTheme is reactive â€” it re-evaluates when the theme changes.
```

## Key points

| Concern | How it works |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Reacts to `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` automatically |
| **No manual subscription** | Unlike `RequestedThemeChanged` event, `AppThemeBinding` is declarative |
| **Resources** | You can also use it inside `ResourceDictionary` styles to apply globally |

### Tip â€” use styles for reuse

```xml
<ContentPage.Resources>
    <Style TargetType="Label">
        <Setter Property="TextColor"
                Value="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />
    </Style>
</ContentPage.Resources>
```

This avoids repeating the binding on every label. Both approaches (XAML and C#) are fully reactive and require no manual theme-change handling.
